generic_rules: Focus repairs on the contract itself, not the underlying architecture.
Use visible AADL features and declared helpers within the AGREE annex.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` keywords for contract elements.
Define range checks using boolean expressions: `eq range_ok : bool = value >= lower and value <= upper;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new architecture to fix AGREE errors; focus on contract adjustments.
Avoid using implementation-specific details in component type contracts.
Do not use undefined or invalid AGREE type names (use `int`, `real`, or `bool`).
